channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Improving LLMs via Validator-to-Generator Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2607.02668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are inconsistent: varying prompts or including unrelated information can lead to unexpected changes in model outputs. The generator-validator (G-V) gap is one manifestation of this phenomenon, where LLMs generate responses that t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, alignment, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PraMem: Practice-derived Experiential Memory for Long-horizon Behavior Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2607.02881v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-horizon behavior prediction aims to infer a user's next action based on a lengthy historical sequence, playing a crucial role in artificial intelligence field. The rise of large language models (LLMs) offers a promising direction for sequential b...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, compression, memory, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SwarmResearch: Orchestrating Coding Agents for Open-Ended Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2607.02807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-running coding agents such as autoresearch can persistently discover optimizations for open-ended problems. However, they tend to converge onto a single high-level approach, then proceed with low-level edits while missing other superior approache...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, arxiv, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Auditing the Audit: Five Failure Modes in Benchmark-Validity Audits
item.description: arXiv:2607.02586v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Governance frameworks ask AI providers and auditors for documented evaluation evidence, and perturbation-based construct-validity audits are a common form of that evidence. We argue the audits are themselves fragile: their conclusions can be silently ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, study, framework, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: QuantFlow: A Federated Mamba-Based Post-Transformer Foundation Model for Time-Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.02632v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time-series forecasting supports decisions in finance, en-ergy, transportation, public health, and industrial monitoring. Recent foundation models improve transfer across forecast-ing tasks, but many depend on centralized data and Trans-former attenti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, attention, arxiv, embedding, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Post-Generation Curation of Synthetic Images via Homogeneous-Heterogeneous Splitting
item.description: arXiv:2607.02637v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent generative models can produce high-quality synthetic images, offering scalable training training data for data-hungry models. Existing approaches to exploiting this potential typically involve 1) training or fine-tuning generators, or 2) using ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, prompt, alignment, image | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LuxSQA: Ask Me in Luxembourgish with TTS-Augmented Spoken Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2607.02763v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spoken Question Answering (SQA) remains largely focused on high-resource languages and carefully recorded speech, limiting the reach of speech-LLM methods in low-resource settings. This paper investigates whether text-to-speech (TTS) can provide task-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, audio, augmented, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: VERITAS: Towards a General-Purpose Replication Tool for Scientific Research
item.description: arXiv:2607.02931v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI tools are accelerating scientific publication while the systems that review it struggle to keep up, and independent verification of published research has become both harder and more important. As manual replication is slow and expensive, a growing...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, research, arxiv, tool, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Weighted Conformal Prediction for Lab-to-Track Thermal Transfer in EV Motorsport Powertrains
item.description: arXiv:2607.02722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predicting thermal volatility in high-performance EV powertrains is difficult as internal temperatures are rarely observable outside the lab, and models calibrated on lab drive cycles fail when deployed against real-world loads. We study this lab-to-t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, tool, study, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Out-of-Distribution Generalization of Risk Aversion in Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.02755v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Training AIs to be risk-averse in resources could offer a failsafe in the event that AIs turn out misaligned. Misaligned but risk-averse AIs would tend to prefer low-risk, low-reward strategies like cooperation over high-risk, high-reward strategies l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, alignment, experiment, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Sliding-Window-Based Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Assembly Flow Shop Scheduling with Multi-Product Delivery
item.description: arXiv:2607.02941v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-product kitting delivery imposes significant challenges for real-time scheduling in hybrid manufacturing systems that integrate processing and assembly, as dynamic order arrivals simultaneously alter supply dependencies and the set of feasible j...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, product, paper, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: LiNO: Lifting based multiresolution neural operator
item.description: arXiv:2607.02715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recently, neural operators have shown promising outcomes for learning solution operators of differential equations directly from data. This framework learns a functional mapping from the parameter field to the solution field, enabling the prediction o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, study, framework, CoT | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
